story.description: A clip of fungi, mushrooms, mycelium, or forest decomposition: close up view of a fungi puffball
story.hook: Mushrooms connect through threads under the soil.
story.lead: Mushrooms connect through threads under the soil. Watch the mushroom cap, then imagine the hidden part below it. Mycelium spreads through soil as fine threads, moving nutrients while breaking down dead material. That is why the real fungal story is usually underground. Which tiny network next?

story.description: A clip of connected habitats, biodiversity, food webs, or ecosystem interactions: close up of hoopoe in natural habitat
story.hook: Coral reefs are built by tiny living builders.

story.description: A close clip of insects: close up of long horned beetle on balcony
story.hook: Beetles can use the night sky like a compass.
story.lead: Beetles can use the night sky like a compass. Watch the straight path, because some dung beetles use sky patterns to keep from circling back into rivals. That celestial shortcut helps a tiny insect roll food away fast. Would you expect a beetle to use astronomy?

reasons: script does not expose enough concrete visual detail
